response.justification: Bogdan Szabo demonstrates strong full-stack and geospatial experience with open-source contributions, but his background lacks deep focus on C++/Rust for routing pipelines or distributed systems at scale. His geospatial work (GISCollective) and low-latency systems experience (Sociomantic) are relevant, but the role's core requirements (C++/Rust, navigation systems) are not prominently featured in his recent work.
strengths: Extensive geospatial experience with GISCollective, including open-source contributions and real-world adoption (e.g., Green Map).
Proven ability to build low-latency, high-throughput systems (e.g., Sociomantic's micro-services handling millions of requests/sec).
Strong problem-solving and experimentation mindset, evidenced by workshops, talks, and tooling projects (e.g., Fluent-Asserts, Trial).
concerns: Limited recent experience with C++ or Rust, the primary languages for the role (last C++ work was in 2015).
Most recent roles focus on full-stack (TypeScript/Node.js) or AI-agent infrastructure, diverging from the role's core requirements.
No explicit experience with navigation guidance systems or routing data pipelines, which are critical for the position.
